title: dryopteris subglandulosa
intro: Dryopteris subglandulosa, also known as the silver fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It is a medicinal plant that has been used by the indigenous Maori people for centuries to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The leaves of the silver fern are traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
